rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE CONNIE MARY MARATAS TRUST 1997; AND THE CONNIE M. MARATAS REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST 2014
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/26/26 to be reviewed (ultimately) with the second and final/supplemental accounting in the conservatorship case, when that accounting petition is filed. In the meantime, the following issues posted in the tentative ruling for the hearing on 8/27/25 still have not been addressed: (1) No proof of service of a correct, complete notice has been filed yet, showing that notice, with a copy of the instructions for how to appear remotely attached to it, was mailed at least 30 days before the hearing on the fourth amended first account. (2) The "notice of the irrevocability" attached to the "Response" filed on 11/6/24 did not meet all the requirements of Probate Code Section 16061.7, including subsections (g) and (h). (3) The 2014 trust had a Schedule A attached to it, which included the real property in San Francisco and in Sebastopol. The successor trustee named in that trust is Madeline Mulky (or Beatrice Gardner). Florence Seebach is not named as either a successor trustee or a beneficiary under that trust. Deadline is 1/12/26 for providing the assigned examiner courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues. Additionally, no estate appears to have been opened yet for the deceased settlor. Whoever is in possession of the will must lodge it [Probate Code Section 8200(a)] and an interested party must initiate the decedent's estate proceedings. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ANNA WONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/8/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) A petition to determine heirs must be filed and adjudicated before a petition for final distribution will be considered. [LRSF 14.35 H.] (A "supplement" by the attorney is not sufficient. Furthermore, a Court-appointed fiduciary must sign and verify his own pleadings, not his attorney. [Prob. Code Sect. 1023 and CRC 7.103(c).]) (2) The amended petition was not e-filed until 11/10/25. Notice may not be mailed before the pleading requiring notice is filed (e-filed). [LRSF 14.14. A.] (3) The amended petition has not been fully reviewed at this time, because of the issues above; however, there are several post-deceased heirs. It is unclear how their successors-in-interest were determined. Have estates not been opened for any of them? A certified copy of Letters issued to a personal representative or certification of trust by a successor trustee must be filed, where applicable. Probate Code Section 13100 affidavits must be filed if no probate proceeding has been initiated for any post-deceased heir. The affidavits must be filed conventionally with the original certified copy of the death certificate attached. Please provide courtesy copies of the any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 3/25/26. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARY M. CARTER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The judicial officer is to review and determine whether Probate Code section 6120(a) should apply to the decedent's codicil to revoke not only the second sentence, but also the first sentence of paragraph fifth of the will that named Salvation Army as the sole residuary devisee. If the codicil operated to revoke paragraph fifth of the will in its entirety including the residuary clause, should the doctrine of dependent relative revocation apply to save the residuary clause? PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROBERT JOHN GEARY
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/28/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No receipt has been filed for the reported distribution of the pecuniary bequest to Patricia A. Geary. (2) Receipts must be filed by Theresa Sanchez, Devin Sancez, Ashley Rezentes, and David Rezentes, showing what each received. An email from someone in the company claiming that someone else in the company confirmed that the checks cleared is insufficient. The email does not even show what or how much was distributed to the beneficiaries. (3) Paragraph 8 of the petition shows the incorrect appraisal value of the [real] property as $2,360,000. With the additional amount shown in the Corrected I&A, the corrected appraisal of the real property was $2,050,000. (4) The costs advanced by the executor are not fully itemized on Attachment 14g to the petition; there should be four columns, showing the date of the payment, the payee, the description, and the amount. (5) The Police Credit Union creditor's claim was $4,652.58, but the executor (not "Administrator") paid $4,667.58. (6) The I&A value used in paragraph 15 to calculate the statutory fee is incorrect. (7) The petition gives a range of fees for the attorney's extraordinary fee in paragraph 23, which is inappropriate. Some of the time entries appear to be ordinary services or quite high: dealing with creditors' claims or reviewing local rules are ordinary services; and 2.25 hours of attorney time at $550 to draft a letter to the court with courtesy copies to increase bond seems rather high, when a paralegal or secretary could have drafted such a letter. (8) There was really just real property in the estate to administer, given that the only other asset (the investment account) was specifically devised. How does the statutory fee not adequately compensate the attorney? [CRC 7.703(a).] Attachment 3 to the supplement filed on 10/6/24, which should have been attachment 16 to the petition, does not show how the attorney time benefited the estate. (9) The prayer requests a "preliminary" distribution, but this is a petition for final distribution. (10) Please show the calculation of the net amounts to be distributed. They do not appear to be correct. Please provide a revised proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 1/14/26. An AMENDMENT TO THE PETITION would be appropriate in order to set forth the correct statutory and actual (not estimated) extraordinary fees and the correct net distribution to each beneficiary. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Item 4 of an I&A should NOT be completed when a probate referee HAS BEEN appointed. The probate referee was appointed on 3/12/25. Please read the whole sentence, not just the prepositional phrase. The persons entitled to notice of this hearing are beneficiaries, not heirs-at-law.) NB: In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate and distribute an estate must BEGIN with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution." [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.] The rest of the order caption should disclose the relief being granted, as with a petition caption.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HAROLD J. MILLER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing for the sole purpose of admitting the will dated 8/17/2004. [Probate Code Section 8225.] No appearance is required. Status hearing will be set for 5/24/27. If a petition for final distribution or a report of status with a petition requesting additional time for estate administration is filed at least two weeks before that status hearing date, no appearance will be required on that date.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HOWARD PARKER JOHNSTON, JR.
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/29/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) An amended pleading or an amendment to a pleading requires the same notice of hearing (including publication) as the pleading it amends. [CRC 7.53(a).] (2) The correct form of notice for a Petition for Letters of Administration is the mandatory Judicial Council form Notice of Petition to Administer Estate. (3) How is the petitioner qualified to search for heirs? The date of Decedent's birth is not even or set forth (or known?) in the family trees. Asking the maternal heirs for information about paternal heirs is not authoritative (and they have no incentive to provide such information in any event). Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned examiner by 12/15/25 to avoid a continuance. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE LEE FAMILY TRUST A, UDT LEE FAMILY TRUST, DATED JULY 26, 1996 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Confirm Assets To Trust And Modify Trust
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/22/25. Examiner's Notes emailed to counsel with the following issues: 1. Please allege where the trustee resides and provide the Probate Code 16061.7 Notice. 2. Please allege whether the bank account and burial contract were in existence at the time the trust and general assignment of interest were created. 3. The waiver of notice states that the parties are entitled to 15-day notice, which is incorrect (30-day notice is required). 4. Please resubmit the order without exhibits, incorporating the information into the body of the order.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE SOFIE KRITIKAKOS TRUST DATED MARCH 22, 1994
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Appointing Successor Trustee; And Petition For Order Ascertaining Beneficiaries Of Trust
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/11/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) No proof of service of notice has been filed. (3) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (4) Since the petitioner is the administrator of the estate of the sole beneficiary, all heirs of his estate are parties entitled to notice. If a proposed order and courtesy copies of the petition and any papers filed to address these issues are not received by the assigned examiner by 2/25/26, the hearing will have to be continued further, as the examiner will be unavailable from 3/9/26 - 3/25/26. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
